queue.title: Finish bounded IDA* with ZeroHeuristic
queue.prompt: Finish and harden the current bounded IDA* implementation using the existing SearchSolution and Heuristic abstractions. Start with ZeroHeuristic, keep search bounded by max_depth, expand only FACE_MOVES, and verify returned moves solve the submitted cube.
acceptance: solved cube returns an empty solution
1-move and 2-move scrambles solve within the depth limit
insufficient max_depth returns None
explored_nodes is reported
the returned solution actually solves the cube
the public search exports are available from cube-engine
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Wire IDA* to simple heuristics
queue.prompt: Allow IDA* to use the existing simple Heuristic implementations without weakening ZeroHeuristic behavior. Keep the public API minimal and add tests showing heuristic-guided IDA* still returns verified solutions for shallow scrambles.
acceptance: IDA* can run with any current Heuristic implementation
MaxHeuristic or equivalent simple heuristic solves shallow scrambles
ZeroHeuristic tests still pass
returned solutions use FACE_MOVES and solve the cube
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Parse Kociemba facelet strings
queue.prompt: Add a user-facing parser for 54-character Kociemba-style facelet strings using U, R, F, D, L, and B symbols. Validate length, allowed symbols, and exact symbol counts. Keep this as an adapter; do not replace CubieState as the engine representation.
acceptance: the canonical solved facelet string parses successfully
invalid length returns a useful error
invalid symbols return a useful error
invalid face counts return a useful error
parsed facelets are exported as an adapter type, not a primary solver state
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Render cubie states as facelets
queue.prompt: Add a facelet serializer for valid Cube or CubieState values that renders the same 54-character Kociemba-style format. Use it for user I/O and tests without changing cubie validation rules.
acceptance: solved cubie state renders to the canonical solved facelet string
states produced by applying moves render without panicking
rendered strings parse through the facelet parser
the serializer does not weaken CubieState validation
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Convert solved facelets to cubies
queue.prompt: Add the first facelet-to-CubieState conversion path for the canonical solved facelet string. Establish explicit corner and edge sticker-position mappings that later arbitrary conversion can reuse.
acceptance: the solved facelet string converts to CubieState::solved
conversion uses explicit corner and edge mapping tables
conversion errors are structured and display useful messages
Cube::try_from_state accepts the converted solved state
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Convert and validate arbitrary facelets
queue.prompt: Extend facelet conversion to arbitrary valid 3x3 states. Identify corner and edge cubies from stickers, compute orientation and permutation, then reuse CubieState validation for uniqueness, orientation sums, and parity.
acceptance: valid moved states convert to CubieState
unknown corner sticker combinations are rejected
unknown edge sticker combinations are rejected
impossible orientation or parity states are rejected through CubieState validation
errors identify the failing piece or validation category
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Add facelet round-trip fixtures
queue.prompt: Add deterministic fixtures that apply known algorithms, render states to facelets, parse and convert them back, and verify the recovered CubieState matches the original. Include invalid-state fixtures for malformed or impossible stickers.
acceptance: known 1-move, 2-move, and 3-move states round-trip through facelets
round-trip tests compare cubie states rather than sticker internals
at least one impossible swapped-piece facelet input is rejected
at least one invalid orientation or parity fixture is rejected

queue.title: Define public solver result types
queue.prompt: Add a solver module with public configuration, result, metrics, and error types. Keep the types independent from any single search algorithm and prepare them for both CubieState and facelet inputs.
acceptance: SolverConfig supports max_depth and max_nodes or documents unsupported limits
SolveResult reports moves, length, and explored_nodes
SolveError distinguishes invalid input from not-found-within-limits
new types are exported from cube-engine without changing existing APIs
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Solve validated cubie states
queue.prompt: Add a public Rust solve API that accepts CubieState or Cube, validates it, runs the best available deterministic solver, and verifies the returned moves by replaying them before returning success.
acceptance: solved CubieState returns an empty solution
known shallow CubieState scrambles return valid solutions
invalid CubieState returns validation errors instead of panicking
returned moves are replayed internally before success
solution metrics include length and explored_nodes
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Solve user facelet states
queue.prompt: Add a public solve API that accepts a 54-character facelet string, parses and validates it, converts it to CubieState, solves through the cubie solver API, and returns structured validation or search errors.
acceptance: solved facelet input returns an empty solution
known shallow facelet input returns a verified solution
invalid length, symbols, counts, and impossible states return useful errors

queue.title: Report solver limits honestly
queue.prompt: Make solver limits explicit and observable. Respect configured max_depth and max_nodes where supported, represent not-found-within-limits separately from invalid input, and avoid unsupported optimality or 20-move guarantees.
acceptance: insufficient max_depth returns not-found-within-limits
max_nodes is enforced or explicitly reported as unsupported by the selected search mode
invalid input errors are distinct from search failures
metrics include enough data for UI display and test assertions
documentation and comments avoid unsupported optimality claims
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Scaffold web application
